article_title: Transparency obligations for certain AI systems
obligation_text: Providers of AI systems, including general-purpose AI systems, generating synthetic audio, image, video or text content, shall ensure that the outputs of the AI system are marked in a machine-readable format and detectable as artificially generated or manipulated.
scoring_text: Skill in implementing AI content provenance marking, including machine-readable format selection for synthetic content identification, content authenticity metadata design, and detection mechanism implementation for artificially generated or manipulated content.
_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Skill in implementing AI content provenance marking, including machine-readable format selection for synthetic content identification, content authenticity metadata design, and detection mechanism implementation for artificially generated or manipulated content.
reference_document_element_description: Ability to assess and communicate the security implications of emerging AI technologies, including foundation models, autonomous agents, and AI-generated content, to both technical and executive audiences.
comments: EU AI Act Art. 50(2) (obligation) intersects with WIDAI SP domain. SP-A-003 is the strongest match (STS: 0.467, cosine: 0.572). Secondary: SP-T-010 (SP, cosine: 0.566), RC-S-008 (RC, cosine: 0.549).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression. Primary method for all elements. Scores the competency_implication text (workforce competency interpretation of the regulatory requirement) against WIDAI KSA statements.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.7/10 (raw 0.467). Shared vocabulary: [ai, content, generated]. EU AI Act competency implication terms [artificially, authenticity, design, detection, format, identification] vs WIDAI terms [ability, agents, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate] define the semantic distance.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.867 (P=0.861, R=0.873). Balanced P/R — the 29-word competency implication and 26-word WIDAI KSA cover each other's token-level semantic content symmetrically.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference classification. NLI entailment is systematically low for competency→competency pairs (mean ~0.007) because NLI measures strict logical implication, not semantic relatedness. Primary diagnostic value is in detecting contradiction.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (100.0%). Standard result for cross-framework competency mapping — the EU AI Act element and WIDAI KSA describe overlapping competencies without strict logical implication.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.572 > STS 0.467 (gap +0.105). Independent encoding finds more similarity than cross-attention. Shared surface terms [ai, content, generated] inflate the bi-encoder, but cross-attention detects semantic divergence beneath the vocabulary overlap.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 11.4%. Shared tokens: [ai, content, generated]. EU AI Act-only: [artificially, authenticity, design, detection, format, identification]. WIDAI-only: [ability, agents, assess, audiences, autonomous, communicate].
